title: feat(insights): send events from hits and refinementList (2/4)
body:
<!--
  Thanks for submitting a pull request!
  Please provide enough information so that others can review your pull request.
-->

## Summary

This PR improves `insights` middleware to send events.
This PR also
- exposes `sendEvent` to `connectRefinementList`
- exposes `sendEvent` and `bindEvent` to `connectHits`
- exposes `bindEvent` to `Hits` and its temlate
to demonstrate how in practice it could work.

[example](https://codesandbox.io/s/instantsearchjs-c9s30?file=/src/app.js) → It works when you check something in the `refinementList` and click any buttons in the `hits` and the custom hits widgets. (You can see the network panel of your browser to inspect the events sent to Algolia Insights)

**Stacked PRs**
1. [introduce `insights` middleware](https://github.com/algolia/instantsearch.js/pull/4446)
2. [send events from hits and refinementList](https://github.com/algolia/instantsearch.js/pull/4456) ← current
3. [implement sendEvent in all the connectors](https://github.com/algolia/instantsearch.js/pull/4463)
4. [add tests](https://github.com/algolia/instantsearch.js/pull/4464)

## Two cases

1. The facet related connectors will run `sendEvent('click', ...)` along with `refine` and expose `sendEvent` to component layer.
2. The hit related connectors will run `sendEvent('view', ...)` when rendering items and expose `sendEvent` to component layer. It will also expose `bindEvent` to templates which will be handled by insightsListener.

We expose `instantSearchInstance.sendEventToInsights()`:
- which is called in connectors
- which can be used by users when they manually want to send events

## Details about the design

<details>
<summary>How to let users sendEvent from `hits` and `infiniteHits`?</summary>

### `click` or `conversion` events from `connectHits`

We need to provide a way for users to send `click` or `conversion` events.

#### Case 1. custom widget

Users can create custom components by using connectors:

```js
let listener;
const renderFn = (renderOptions) => {
  const { hits, sendEvent } = renderOptions;

  const elem = /* ... */;
  if (listener) {
    elem.removeEventListener("click", listener);
  }
  listener = (event) => {
    const index = /* ... */;
    const hit = hits[index];
    sendEvent('conversion', hit, "Product Bookmarked");  // or 'click'
    // ↑ I've got feedback from Mathieu that we cannot use default event names for 'click' and 'conversion' in the context of hits.
    // It's because there can be many different types of clicks and conversions. Moreover the event names are used by Perso. So we should leave it to the users.
  }
  elem.addEventListener("click", listener);
};
const customWidget = connectHits(renderFn);
```

And they can use `sendEvent` exposed by the connector. So this it looks okay in this case.

#### Case 2. template

`hits` and `infiniteHits` widgets are normally used with the `item` template.

```js
templates: {
  item(hit) {
    const html = /* ... */;
    return html;
  },
},
```

In this case, even if we expose `sendEvent` as in `item(hit, sendEvent, eventName)`, it's not easy to use because at this stage we don't have DOM element to attach event listeners.

And even if we try to add event listeners outside the InstantSearch lifecycle,

```js
search.addWidgets([
  hits({
    container: "#hits",
    templates: {
      item(hit) {
        /* ... */
      },
    },
  }),
]);

document.querySelector("#hits").addEventListener("click", (event) => {
  // How do we get `hit` object here?
});
```

So, in the end, we go back to what we've been doing:

```js
templates: {
  item: (hit) => `
    <button ${instantsearch.insights("clickedObjectIDsAfterSearch", {
      eventName: "click-result",
      objectIDs: [hit.objectID],
    })}>
      Click event
    </button>
  `;
}
```

Similarly,

```js
templates: {
  item: (hit, bindEvent) => `
    <button ${bindEvent("click", hit, "Product Bookmarked")}>
      Click event
    </button>
  `;
}
```

`bindEvent` is similar to `sendEvent`, but instead of delivering it to `insights` middleware, it returns a string like the following:

```js
return `data-insights-event="${btoa(JSON.stringify({ widgetType, eventType, aaMethod, payload ))}"`
```

And we can extend [insightsListener](https://github.com/algolia/instantsearch.js/blob/master/src/lib/insights/listener.tsx#L26:L26) to handle this data- attribute.

According to this addition, I'm in favor of deprecating the old way of

```js
    <button ${instantsearch.insights("clickedObjectIDsAfterSearch", {
      eventName: "click-result",
      objectIDs: [hit.objectID],
    })}>
      Click event
    </button>
```
and things related to it (for example, `withInsights` and `withInsightsListener`).
</details>

<details>
<summary>Sending custom events</summary>

If users want to send raw custom event without any implication, they can call `search.sendEventToInsights(args)` and `args` will be sent to `insights` middleware if being used.
The minimum `args` for Algolia Insights is `{ aaMethod, payload }`. The full signature is `{ widgetType, eventType, aaMethod, payload }`.

</details>

<details>
<summary>Default event names</summary>

### Facet
We normally send only `click` event from these connectors: connectRefinementList, connectBreadcrumb, ...
And its default event name is `Filter Applied`, which makes sense.

### Hit
Here we could have all of three types of events:
- view
- click
- conversion

In case of `view`, we can safely use `Hits Viewed` as a default event name.

However in case of `click` or `conversion`, we cannot force any default event name because there can be different types of events and if we use a default name for all, it can affect the quality of personalization. So we do not have any default name and let users decide.
</details>
